title: Volunteer Opportunities * Homelessness Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Volunteering & Donating > Volunteer Opportunities * Homelessness Issues 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter Autism Spectrum Disorder Child Abuse Issues Disaster/Emergency Issues Domestic Violence Issues Foster Children Homelessness Issues Seasonal Workers Veterans Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AGAPE VILLAGE CENTRAL NAZARENE UGM VOLUNTEERING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF For community members interested in becoming volunteers at Central Nazarene UGM overnight shelter. 9715 SE Powell Boulevard Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, must pass a background check Hours: 7 days a week 6:30am-8am and 9pm-11pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details AGAPE VILLAGE ST STEPHENS OPERATION NIGHTWATCH VOLUNTEERING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF For community members interested in becoming volunteers at Central Nazarene UGM overnight shelter. 1432 SW 13th Avenue Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, must pass a background check Hours: 7 days a week 6:30am-8am and 9pm-11pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO DONATIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Accepts donations of camping gear (tents and sleeping bags), clothing (socks, underwear, shoes, men’s and women’s apparel), bedding (pillows and blankets), toiletries (soap, shampoo, conditioner, toothbrushes, toothpaste, deodorant, body wipes), food (cooked or uncooked), and operational supplies (55-gallon trash bags, paper plates, plastic cutlery). Cash or check donations can be made onsite to support utility and material needs; receipts are available at the front desk. Financial donations may also be mailed to the registered office or made online. Also advocates for policies addressing homelessness and offers volunteer opportunities. 999 N Thunderbird Way Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Saturday 8am-10pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details AGAPE VILLAGE VOLUNTEERING AGAPE VILLAGE VOLUNTEERING RIGHT 2 DREAM TOO DONATIONS
